title: viola arenaria subsp. rupestris
intro: Viola arenaria subsp. rupestris, also known as the rock or mountain pansy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family. It is native to Europe and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and cardiovascular protective effects. The herbal preparations of Viola arenaria subsp. rupestris include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations.
